allocations.schema:
CREATE TABLE allocations (
                    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    client TEXT DEFAULT 'adani',
                    member_name TEXT NOT NULL COLLATE NOCASE,
                    project_name TEXT NOT NULL COLLATE NOCASE,
                    hours REAL DEFAULT 0,
                    UNIQUE(client, member_name, project_name)
                )

attendance.schema:
CREATE TABLE attendance (
                client TEXT DEFAULT 'adani',
                member_name TEXT COLLATE NOCASE,
                date TEXT,
                check_in TEXT,
                check_out TEXT,
                work_mode TEXT,
                daily_hrs REAL,
                project_breakdown TEXT,
                total_hours REAL DEFAULT 0,
                PRIMARY KEY (client, member_name, date)
            )

rows.project_breakdown: {"IoT Asset Monitoring & Management Platform":8,"Vehicle Entry Management Platform":0,"Visitor Management System (PVMS)":0}

audit_logs.schema:
CREATE TABLE audit_logs (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            occurred_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
            actor_username TEXT,
            actor_name TEXT,
            actor_role TEXT,
            client TEXT DEFAULT 'adani',
            action TEXT NOT NULL,
            entity_type TEXT NOT NULL,
            entity_id TEXT,
            entity_name TEXT,
            before_state TEXT,
            after_state TEXT,
            metadata TEXT DEFAULT '{}'
        )

rows.entity_name: RBI Notification Intelligence Pipeline
rows.before_state: {"brd_pdf_file": "", "burned_story_pts": 24.0, "business_user": "", "client": "adani", "custom_metrics": "{}", "department": "", "description": "Imported from RBI_Agent_TaskSheet.xlsx.", "end_date": "2025-08-21", "est_hours": 576.0, "hours_burned": 576.0, "id": 44, "name": "RBI Notification Intelligence Pipeline", "owner": "", "pdd_pdf_file": "", "sprint": "", "start_date": "2025-08-01", "status": "Completed", "tech_stack": "", "total_story_pts": 24.0}
rows.after_state:
{"brd_pdf_file": "", "burned_story_pts": 24.0, "business_user": "Pragnesh Darji", "client": "adani", "custom_metrics": "{}", "department": "Secretarial", "description": "The Business Challenge
In large conglomerates (such as Adani Group), regulatory compliance is a critical business function. The compliance and legal teams must monitor the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) notification portal daily. The manual workflow is highly inefficient and error-prone:

Manually visiting the RBI portal multiple times a day.
Checking for new notifications, circulars, and master directions.
Downloading PDFs, reading dense legal text, and determining business relevance.
Extracting key takeaways while ensuring no Personal Identifiable Information (PII) is leaked.
Composing summary emails and distributing them along with Excel reports to various business unit heads.
Missing a single critical circular could lead to severe regulatory penalties, operational risk, or reputational damage.

The Solution: Autonomous Compliance Pipeline
The goal of this project was to design, implement, and deploy an autonomous, end-to-end AI-driven pipeline that automates the entire lifecycle of RBI notification monitoring. The pipeline runs within the corporate network behind a strict proxy (cloudproxy.adani.com:8080) and performs:

Playwright web scraping to identify new circulars.
Idempotent PDF downloads with SHA-256 deduplication.
Boilerplate and PII sanitization using Python and regular expressions.
Compliance-grade summarization using a local Microsoft Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct Small Language Model (SLM) running on an NVIDIA Tesla T4 GPU.
Dual Persistence to a local SQLite VectorStore (with embeddings for semantic search) and Azure PostgreSQL (for cloud business intelligence).
Automated reporting and distribution via SMTP.
Observability and alerting to log run metrics, execution times, and catch infrastructure/network errors.", "end_date": "2025-08-21", "est_hours": 576.0, "hours_burned": 576.0, "id": 44, "name": "RBI Notification Intelligence Pipeline", "owner": "Abhishek Mane", "pdd_pdf_file": "", "sprint": "", "start_date": "2025-08-01", "status": "New", "tech_stack": "Python, FastAPI, Playwright, PyMuPDF, Docling, SQLite, Azure PostgreSQL, PyTorch, Sentence-Transformers, Microsoft Phi-3 Mini", "total_story_pts": 24.0}
rows.metadata: {"summary": "Saved project 'RBI Notification Intelligence Pipeline'."}

rows.entity_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.before_state:
{"brd_pdf_file": "", "burned_story_pts": 50.0, "business_user": "Pragnesh Darji", "client": "adani", "custom_metrics": "{}", "department": "Secretarial", "description": "The project builds an automated AI-powered pipeline to streamline tracking of BSE filings and financial data. It replaces manual effort by:

Scraping filings using Playwright
Extracting data from scanned/unstructured PDFs with OCR
Using LLMs (Azure Llama-4 & Cohere) for smart summarization
Storing data centrally in PostgreSQL (replacing Excel/SQLite silos)
Generating automated reports with charts and email delivery
Adding AI quality checks to prevent hallucinations

Goal: Reduce manual workload, improve accuracy, enable real-time insights, and deliver faster, reliable reporting for analysts and fund managers.", "end_date": "2025-08-30", "est_hours": 400.0, "hours_burned": 400.0, "id": 48, "name": "BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline", "owner": "Abhishek Mane", "pdd_pdf_file": "", "sprint": "", "start_date": "2025-07-29", "status": "New", "tech_stack": "Python, FastAPI, Microsoft Phi-3 Mini, Playwright, PyMuPDF, Docling, SQLite, Azure PostgreSQL, PyTorch, Sentence-Transformers", "total_story_pts": 50.0}

rows.entity_name: RBI Notification Intelligence Pipeline
rows.before_state:
{"brd_pdf_file": "", "burned_story_pts": 24.0, "business_user": "Pragnesh Darji", "client": "adani", "custom_metrics": "{"Deployment Status": "live"}", "department": "Secretarial", "description": "In large conglomerates (such as Adani Group), regulatory compliance is a critical business function. The compliance and legal teams must monitor the **Reserve Bank of India (RBI)** notification portal daily. The manual workflow is highly inefficient and error-prone:
1. Manually visiting the RBI portal multiple times a day.
2. Checking for new notifications, circulars, and master directions.
3. Downloading PDFs, reading dense legal text, and determining business relevance.
4. Extracting key takeaways while ensuring no Personal Identifiable Information (PII) is leaked.
5. Composing summary emails and distributing them along with Excel reports to various business unit heads.

Missing a single critical circular could lead to severe regulatory penalties, operational risk, or reputational damage.", "end_date": "2025-08-21", "est_hours": 192.0, "hours_burned": 192.0, "id": 47, "name": "RBI Notification Intelligence Pipeline", "owner": "Abhishek Mane", "pdd_pdf_file": "", "sprint": "", "start_date": "2025-08-01", "status": "New", "tech_stack": "Python, FastAPI, Microsoft Phi-3 Mini, Playwright, PyMuPDF, Docling, SQLite, Azure PostgreSQL, PyTorch, Sentence-Transformers", "total_story_pts": 24.0}

rows.meeting_title: RAC Project – Team Responsibilities and Current Progress Update
rows.description:
Dear Team,

I would like to share the current status and role allocation for the RAC Project business team activities.

The project is currently being handled by a team of three members, with responsibilities distributed as follows:

Rahul Chenna – Working on the implementation and development of the remaining graph outputs in Excel using the existing OEM data files. This includes preparing and validating the required graphical representations based on project requirements.
Imran – Handling the Windographer-related activities, including integrating the required data files into the tool and capturing graph screenshots/output reports for further project analysis.
Pallavi – Responsible for analyzing and reviewing the data files to ensure data consistency, accuracy, and support for project deliverables.

The team is actively coordinating to ensure timely completion of the project activities and smooth execution of the deliverables.

Please feel free to reach out in case of any further clarifications or additional requirements.

Best Regards,
Rahul Chenna

rows.meeting_title: Discussion on project status, concerns, and future planning and Data source usage
rows.participants: Mehul, Mayank, Vardhan, Prashant, Imran, Pranav
rows.description:
Subject: Resource Assessment Minutes of Meeting (MOM) 26 May 2026 
 
Hello Everyone,
Minutes of Meeting (MOM)
Date: 26 May 2026
Participants: Mehul, Mayank, Vardhan, Prashant, Imran, Pranav, 
Purpose: Discussion on project status, concerns, and future planning
Key Highlights:
 
________________________________________
1. Data Source Analysis (Wind-Solar)
•	SQL database review has been completed.
•	Comparative analysis between raw data files and SQL database has been completed.
•	User confirmed that we can use database for wind for further development
•	User confirmed that satellite data is also stored in consistent format so we can use that database 
Dependency / Blocker:
•	SharePoint access required to review raw data files.
•	Access request has already been shared with users.
________________________________________
2. Windographer Automation
•	Graph generation from raw data for MIS is completed.
•	Automation of file upload into Windographer is completed.
•	Automation for generating time series and wind rose graphs is completed.
•	Automation for downloading graphs / capturing screenshots is completed.
Next Step:
•	Integration to fetch data from database into Windographer.
Dependency / Blocker:
•	Windographer license required (trial expired).
•	License request has been shared with users.
________________________________________
3. Database Development
•	Development database setup is currently in progress.
________________________________________
4. Solar Data Discussion
•	Requested to have a detailed discussion with RA team to capture the calculation logic and methodology for MIS preparation in the solar project.

rows.meeting_title: RA Team Meetings and Progress Update
rows.participants: Rahul, Imran, Nithish, Syed pasha, Pranav, Prashant, Durgesh, Meet,
rows.description:
Dear Team,
Please find below the summary of meetings and progress updates from 16th June to 19th June:
16th June 2026
Conducted a meeting with all RA team users and stakeholders to discuss project requirements, current processes, and overall expectations.
17th June 2026
Held a meeting with the Solar team to discuss the Solar MIS calculation process and the data handling approach required for report generation.
18th June 2026
Attended an in-person meeting with Durgesh and Tomy regarding the approach for proceeding with or without Windographer integration. Imran, Nithish, and Pranav also attended the discussion.
Subsequently, an internal meeting was conducted with Prashant, Syed, and the development team regarding task allocation.
One team was assigned to analyze Windographer activities to understand its functionalities and evaluate the feasibility of automation.
Another team was assigned to analyze the raw data and post-processed data generated through Windographer to identify the transformations and calculations performed before and after processing.
Nithish was assigned to prepare a workflow diagram and MOM (Minutes of Meeting) documentation.
19th June 2026
Received the Windographer license for further analysis and evaluation.
The required data files are still pending from the user and are awaited to continue the detailed assessment and development activities.

rows.meeting_title: RA Team Meetings and Progress Update
rows.participants: Rahul, Imran, Nithish, Syed pasha, Pranav, Prashant, Durgesh, Tomy, Meet
rows.description:
Today, I analyzed the raw files received from the user and compared them with the corresponding post-processed files shared earlier. During the analysis, it was observed that certain filtering rules had been applied to the raw data, resulting in differences between the raw and processed datasets.
Additionally, based on discussions held yesterday and continued today (20-06-2026), the user recommended proceeding with the raw files directly for both Wind and Solar data processing.
Reasoning:
Wind Data: NRG file decryption is currently failing, which prevents complete data insertion into the database and results in data loss.
Solar Data: Variations in column structures across files are causing data discrepancies and data loss during the raw-to-database ingestion process.
To avoid data loss and ensure data integrity, the user suggested moving forward with the raw files as the primary source for further processing and analysis.

rows.name: SEBI Circulars RAG & Summarization Pipeline
rows.description:
In large conglomerates (such as Adani Group), regulatory compliance is a critical business function. The compliance and legal teams must monitor the **Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)** circulars and notifications portal daily. The manual workflow is highly inefficient and error-prone:
1. Manually visiting the SEBI portal multiple times a day.
2. Checking for new circulars, advisories, and policy documents.
3. Downloading PDFs, reading dense legal text, and determining business relevance.
4. Extracting key takeaways while ensuring no Personal Identifiable Information (PII) is leaked.
5. Composing summary emails and distributing them along with Excel reports to various business unit heads.

Missing a single critical circular could lead to severe regulatory penalties, operational risk, or reputational damage.
rows.tech_stack: Python, FastAPI, Microsoft Phi-3 Mini, Playwright, PyMuPDF, Docling, SQLite, Azure PostgreSQL, PyTorch, Sentence-Transformers

rows.name: RBI Notification Intelligence Pipeline
rows.description:
In large conglomerates (such as Adani Group), regulatory compliance is a critical business function. The compliance and legal teams must monitor the **Reserve Bank of India (RBI)** notification portal daily. The manual workflow is highly inefficient and error-prone:
1. Manually visiting the RBI portal multiple times a day.
2. Checking for new notifications, circulars, and master directions.
3. Downloading PDFs, reading dense legal text, and determining business relevance.
4. Extracting key takeaways while ensuring no Personal Identifiable Information (PII) is leaked.
5. Composing summary emails and distributing them along with Excel reports to various business unit heads.

Missing a single critical circular could lead to severe regulatory penalties, operational risk, or reputational damage.
rows.tech_stack: Python, FastAPI, Microsoft Phi-3 Mini, Playwright, PyMuPDF, Docling, SQLite, Azure PostgreSQL, PyTorch, Sentence-Transformers

rows.name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.description:
The project builds an automated AI-powered pipeline to streamline tracking of BSE filings and financial data. It replaces manual effort by:

Scraping filings using Playwright
Extracting data from scanned/unstructured PDFs with OCR
Using LLMs (Azure Llama-4 & Cohere) for smart summarization
Storing data centrally in PostgreSQL (replacing Excel/SQLite silos)
Generating automated reports with charts and email delivery
Adding AI quality checks to prevent hallucinations

Goal: Reduce manual workload, improve accuracy, enable real-time insights, and deliver faster, reliable reporting for analysts and fund managers.
rows.tech_stack: Python, FastAPI, Microsoft Phi-3 Mini, Playwright, PyMuPDF, Docling, SQLite, Azure PostgreSQL, PyTorch, Sentence-Transformers

1. Business logic analysis & architecture design of the modular SEBI ETL-AI pipeline structure
2. Verify GPU capabilities, compile PyTorch with CUDA 12.4, & configure virtual environments
3. Playwright browser automation framework setup and SEBI web scraper initialization
4. Configure Chromium proxy parameters for internal corporate Adani network accessibility
5. Implement dynamic page crawler to parse SEBI dynamic circular tables and extract PDF links
6. Develop secure PDF document downloading module for SEBI circulars with directory auto-creation
7. Establish SHA-256 provenance hash verification to prevent duplicate SEBI circular reprocessing
8. Integrate PyMuPDF library for text extraction of standard compliance circulars
9. Setup Docling structural fallback parsing engine to handle complex multi-column SEBI tables
10. Program a high-performance regex-based text sanitizer to strip boilerplates, signatures & metadata
11. Setup local Microsoft Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct model loading with HuggingFace Transformers
12. Implement float16 model precision quantization to optimize Tesla T4 VRAM overhead to ~8GB
13. Code a recursive chunking summarizer engine to process documents larger than 4K tokens
14. Develop prompt structures & post-inference output safety module (verify_no_leaks) for PII
15. Design local and remote PostgreSQL database schemas to store text logs, metadata, and execution parameters
16. Integrate Sentence Transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) for generating 384-dimensional text vectors
17. Build PostgreSQL-based vector store modules to index text embeddings and perform cosine similarity search
18. Create cloud storage integration module for syncing summaries with Azure PostgreSQL database
19. Implement monthly consolidated openpyxl Excel reporting module with custom grid formatting
20. Build SMTP HTML email notifications routing engine with success reporting rules
21. Program a custom PostgreSQL telemetry Observability module to audit runtime metrics & API errors
22. Configure automatic SMTP administrator email alert mechanisms on critical pipeline exceptions
23. Orchestrate full pipeline process pipeline.py and CLI entrypoint script main.py
24. Conduct end-to-end proxy stress tests, GPU load validations, and final production server release

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 1. Requirements Ingestion & Stakeholder Alignment: Conducted workshops with business analysts and fund managers to map project requirements.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 2. Architecture Blueprinting & Design: Designed the modular RAG pipeline schematic, detailing ingestion, database sync, and SMTP reporting modules.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 3. Tech Stack & Tooling Feasibility Analysis: Researched local Phi-3 execution vs. Azure AI endpoints, choosing a hybrid architecture.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 4. Python Virtual Environment provisioning: Configured environment isolation and resolved package dependencies.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 5. GPU Hardware Capability Verification: Audited workstation GPU memory and compiled/installed PyTorch for CUDA 12.4 compatibility.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 6. Git Version Control & Repository Setup: Initialized Git repository, established branching workflows, and created boilerplate project templates.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 7. Credentials & Secret Management System: Programmed environment variables loader (.env) to handle database credentials and API keys securely.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 8. Playwright Framework Setup: Initialized the Playwright automation framework and Chromium browser launcher.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 9. Corporate Network Proxy Resolution: Configured proxy parameters for Adani internal network access in Playwright.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 10. Stateful DOM Traversal Engine: Coded row-walking traversal selectors to navigate BSE portal and locate announcement lists.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 11. User-Agent Rotation & Obfuscation: Implemented user-agent headers spoofing to make browser scraping mimic human browsing patterns.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 12. Playwright Timeout & Exception Handlers: Programmed recovery rules for network drops, timeout flags, and captcha blocks.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 13. Multi-threaded PDF Ingestion Client: Coded a downloader with ThreadPoolExecutor to download files in parallel.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 14. Date Validation & Ingestion Filter: Programmed date parser filters to verify that files fall strictly within the specified reporting windows.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 15. Document Provenance Cryptographic Hashing: Developed SHA-256 check hashing to record file signatures and prevent duplicate processing.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 16. Digital PDF Text Parser (PyMuPDF): Integrated PyMuPDF (fitz) to extract text and capture layout structure from digital PDFs.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 17. Scanned PDF Layout Detection: Coded image density filters to identify scanned filings that cannot be processed via simple parsing.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 18. OCR Fallback Processing Setup: Configured PyTesseract and OCR engines to render scanned PDF pages into high-resolution images.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 19. Page-limited OCR text extractor: Implemented first-6-page extraction limits to optimize processing speed and skip generic appendices.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 20. Peer Code Review - Scraper Resiliency: Conducted code review sessions focusing on scraping performance and memory leak prevention.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 21. Text Preprocessing & Regex-based Cleaner: Programmed sanitizers to strip legal disclaimers, signatures, and empty line sequences from text.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 22. Unicode Normalizer Integration: Coded character normalize routines to clean scramble texts resulting from extraction anomalies.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 23. PostgreSQL Relational Schema Design: Defined tables (processing_entities, filings_summary) with constraints and indexing in pgsql.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 24. SQLite-to-PostgreSQL Conversion Utility: Wrote migration scripts (migrate_bsedb.py, excel_to_postgres_converter.py) to migrate database data to pgsql.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 25. ChromaDB Vector Store Setup: Integrated ChromaDB for storing and querying text chunks based on semantic embeddings.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 26. Custom Text Chunking with Overlap: Programmed text splitter functions with customizable chunk size and chunk overlap parameters.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 27. Zero-Dependency Vector Store (NumPy): Built a numpy-based cosine similarity search engine to run local searches without external databases.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 28. Database Connection Pooling Implementation: Configured connection pooling to prevent database exhaustion under concurrent threads.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 29. Stakeholder Report Design Workshop: Conducted a design feedback session with business leaders to finalize output report structures.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 30. Architecture Review & Code Alignment: Led a walkthrough session on database mapping rules and vector storage schemas.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 31. Local LLM Ingestion (Phi-3-mini): Programmed loading patterns for Microsoft Phi-3-mini model using HuggingFace Transformers.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 32. Float16 Model Quantization: Applied float16 quantization to optimize GPU VRAM memory usage, lowering resource usage to ~8GB.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 33. Azure AI Inference curl subprocess implementation: Coded a library-free subprocess curl client to connect to Azure Llama-4-Maverick API.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 34. Azure Cohere Embed v4.0 Client: Integrated Azure Cohere Embed REST calls for high-fidelity embedding generation.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 35. Model Loader Toggle Config Setup: Implemented a global toggle config (USE_AZURE_AI) to switch between local and cloud APIs.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 36. Azure API Rate Limit (429) Handling: Coded backoff and retry rules to handle cloud API rate limits under heavy volume.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 37. Prompt Engineering & Prompt tuning: Designed prompts with few-shot context examples to capture financial facts.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 38. PII Safety & Output Filtering: Programmed validation rules (verify_no_leaks) to check LLM outputs for sensitive information.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 39. Technical Training - AI Architecture: Conducted team training sessions on hybrid local/cloud prompt formatting and RAG parameters.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 40. Pair Programming - LLM Inference Troubleshooting: Handled troubleshooting sessions to resolve model parsing failures and timeout errors.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 41. Consolidated Excel Report Formatting: Programmed openpyxl formatting patterns for grids, font alignments, and clickable hyperlinks.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 42. Weekly Trend & Monthly Summary Generator: Wrote logic to compile summary metrics and insert them into structured sheets.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 43. HTML and PNG Stock Charts Integration: Programmed matplotlib and html trend chart generators to visualize monthly volumes.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 44. SMTP Reporting Engine Implementation: Built SMTP email distribution scripts to mail Excel reports with inline trend images.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 45. Distribution list dynamic extractor: Coded excel parsers to load email recipients dynamically from external configuration sheets.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 46. Telemetry Logging & Observability alerts: Programmed database hooks to record runtimes, token counts, and errors in PostgreSQL.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 47. SMTP Alerting on Critical Exceptions: Set up alert mechanisms to email developers detailed exception logs on runtime failures.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 48. RAGAS Quality Metrics Setup: Programmed RAGAS validation scripts to check LLM answers for faithfulness and context recall.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 49. Technical Walkthrough - IT Operations Handover: Led handover and training sessions for the operations team on running the pipeline via cron.

rows.project_name: BSE PDF Intelligence & Market Analytics Pipeline
rows.task_name: 50. End-to-End Stress Testing & Production Deployment: Conducted final proxy validations and deployed the pipeline scheduler to the production server.

team_members.schema:
CREATE TABLE team_members (
                id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                client       TEXT DEFAULT 'adani',
                username     TEXT NOT NULL,
                name         TEXT NOT NULL COLLATE NOCASE,
                designation  TEXT NOT NULL,
                availability INTEGER DEFAULT 8,
                tech         TEXT DEFAULT '',
                location     TEXT DEFAULT '',
                UNIQUE(client, username)
            )

users.schema:
CREATE TABLE users (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            username TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
            password TEXT,
            name TEXT,
            role TEXT DEFAULT 'user',
            vendor_slugs TEXT DEFAULT '[]',
            is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
            created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
        , is_hidden integer default 0, is_team_member_login INTEGER DEFAULT 0, email TEXT DEFAULT '')

vendors.schema:
CREATE TABLE vendors (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            slug TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
            display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
            created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
        , logo_url TEXT DEFAULT '', is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1)
